zombie_page.title: Zombie Siege Chronicles
zombie_page.subtitle: An intense Tower Defense strategy game featuring modular defenses and a custom teleportation system.

about.title: The Last Stand Against the Undead
desc: In a world ravaged by the undead, survival depends on strategic mastery. Zombie Siege Chronicles is a tower defense experience where players must protect their refuge from relentless waves.
This project was developed in a collaborative environment, allowing me to focus on core systemic mechanics, UI architecture, and complex gameplay features like the portal system.
It blends top-down strategy with real-time resource management, rewarding players for efficient defense placement and quick tactical thinking.

gameplay.title: Strategy & Survival
items: • Defensive Arsenal: Deploy a variety of turrets (Robot, Railgun, Shotgun) and barriers to redirect and halt zombie hordes.
• Portal System: A strategic teleportation mechanic allowing the player to instantly move between two custom-placed locations.
• Dynamic Waves: Escalating difficulty with diverse zombie types (Tanks, Runners, Saboteurs) that adapt to the player's defenses.
• Special Events: High-stakes 'Save a Survivor' missions that reward players with health or resources during the onslaught.

tech.title: Systemic Engineering
items: • Portal Logic: Developed the C++/BP logic for portal pairs, including distance-based activation, cooldowns, and seamless teleportation.
• Inventory & Shop: Built a comprehensive UI system for purchasing and managing defenses, integrated with the game's economy.
• UI/UX Architecture: Implemented 3D Main Menus with spline-based camera paths and high-performance in-game HUDs.
• Async Loading: Integrated professional loading screens using asynchronous plugins to maintain high polish during level transitions.

systems.title: Optimization & Polish
items: • Asset Standardization: Enforced naming conventions and project hierarchy to ensure smooth collaboration within the dev team.
• Collision Management: Optimized complex collision channels for overlapping defenses and enemy navigation paths.
• Audio Integration: Designed a dynamic audio system using mixers for environmental immersion and UI feedback.
• Tutorial & UX: Created the 'How to Play' framework and interactive tutorial steps to guide new players through strategy basics.

gallery.architecture_desc: Full architectural map illustrating the relationships between game mode logic, enemy spawners, and the defensive structure hierarchy.

portals.title: Teleportation System
portals.desc: Implementation of the portal pair mechanic. These dual-placed actors allow for rapid player repositioning during intense combat waves.
desc1: Breakdown of the Portal actor: features dynamic materials (in the Entrance Reference mesh), invisible interaction planes for vertical navigation
and a debug arrow to verify entry/exit vectors during development
desc2: The placement system performs real-time surface checks. Here, the portal turns red (invalid state) because the player is attempting to build on the road
where structures are prohibited to ensure pathfinding integrity.
desc3: Successful verification on allowed surfaces (sidewalks). The mesh switches to blue (valid state), providing immediate intuitive feedback.
This logic ensures strategic placement and prevents collision conflicts with the environment.

gallery.ui_desc: The strategic interface showing resource management, wave progression, and the modular shop for buying new turrets.
gallery.menu_desc: A fully interactive 3D environment used for the main menu, featuring a CineCamera rig following a custom spline rail.
gallery.howtoplay_desc: The 'How to Play' widget system designed to introduce diverse zombie types and defensive strengths to the player.
gallery.waves_desc: Back-end logic for wave escalation, managing enemy stat upgrades every four rounds to maintain a constant challenge.
